sentence_en:

"Enter the Grand Battle The new temporary Grand Battle mode places players in an alternate history where ships' main batteries precede aviation on deck."
"Players earn Intelligence Reports — a new type of temporary resource collected daily by logging in for the first time of the day and by playing in the Grand Battle with Tier IX-X ships — to gain the opportunity to command these powerful ships."
"Rewards for participating in dedicated combat missions include Dazzling expendable camos, a commemorative flag, and the Grand Battle patch."
"Celebrating US Independence with Summer Battles With the release of update 0.10.5, four themed groups of combat missions called Summer Battles will guide players to receive, along with camouflages and signal flags, the ultimate reward — the battleship Colorado in its new permanent Independence Day camouflage, alongside a themed auction."

sentence_en:
"One day, the stepmother, who was evil, has the idea of ​​abandoning the children in the middle of the forest, because that way there would be two fewer mouths to feed."
"However, the stepmother became possessed and convinced her husband again to abandon their children in an even further away place."
"So, the witch decides to trap João in a cage and feed him until he is fat enough to be roasted in the oven."
"João managed to trick the witch, who thought he wasn't fat enough, but even so, one day she decides to roast him anyway."
"The witch then orders Maria to light the oven and when the evil old woman comes close to the oven, Maria pushes her inside and quickly closes the lid."
"This time, the father receives them and no longer lets the stepmother do bad things."
"The witch takes them prisoner and the children need to access their inner strength, creativity and courage in order to get out of trouble."
"They give the dagger to the little mermaid and say that she should kill the prince, only then can she have her tail again and return to her home kingdom."
"In this way, people sell him to a circus, where Pinocchio works exhaustively and is treated poorly."
"So, when he, in the form of a donkey, was no longer fit for work, the circus owners threw him into the sea."
"The doll also avoids his responsibilities all the time, straying from the path to school and his chores."
"So, one day, when asking the mirror, the stepmother received the answer: “My queen, you are really very beautiful, but now Snow White is the most beautiful of all.” Furious, the stepmother ordered one of her servants to kidnap Snow White and kill her in the forest, bringing her heart as proof of her death."
"She then decides to disguise herself as an old peasant woman and goes to the forest to offer a poisoned apple to Snow White."
"The stepmother and the new “sisters” treated Cinderella very badly and, after the death of the patriarch, the three of them forced her to sleep in the attic, do all the housework and dress in rags."
"After a while, the beautiful girl was born and soon the sorceress appeared to take her, naming her Rapunzel."
"Rapunzel lived in the witch's house until she was twelve years old, when the evil one decided to imprison her in a huge tower with no doors in the middle of the forest."
"Thus, the witch to climb the tower asked Rapunzel to throw her braids, which served as a large rope."
"Time passed and Rapunzel got used to living very sad and alone, with only the witch visiting."
"One day, the witch sees the prince climbing the tower and decides to take revenge."
"The next morning, the evil one goes to Rapunzel, cuts her hair and abandons her in the forest."
"In Rapunzel, the girl's mother represents an uncontrollable desire for material things, which appears and has no consequences, even sacrificing her daughter, that is, her creative potential."
"She was furious because she wasn't invited and, in revenge, she cast a curse: — When she turns 15, Aurora will find a spinning wheel and prick her finger."
"So she will die."
"Meanwhile, the wolf runs towards the grandmother's house and, once there, swallows the old woman in one fell swoop."
"— It's to eat you better!"
"The hunter then hits the wolf on the head and opens its belly to remove Grandma, who comes out alive."
"They have the idea of ​​filling the animal's belly with lots of stones and sewing it up."
"This is done, and when the wolf wakes up, he is so heavy that he cannot walk and dies."
"This is because it tells the story of a girl and the dangers that surround her, and in the first versions the outcome is terrible, with the death of her grandmother and granddaughter."
"The presence of the wolf tells us about the loss of innocence and the consequences of blindly trusting people with bad intentions."
"Therefore, you need to sharpen your intuition in order to protect yourself."
"The merchant explains that he was picking roses for his daughter, but the Beast still says he is going to kill him."
"The cat jumps on top of the mouse and swallows it in one fell swoop."
"When he arrives, his mother is furious with the change and throws the beans out the window."
comment: O texto é adequado para uma avaliação em larga escala; no entanto, trechos como "O patinho, depois de ser muito humilhado e experimentar sentimentos de angústia, desamparo e baixa auto estima, consegue se dar conta de seu valor.", " A família era bastante humilde e as coisas estavam cada vez mais difíceis para eles, que estavam sem recursos e entrando na miséria.", "Certo dia, a madrasta, que era má, tem a ideia de abandonar as crianças no meio da floresta, pois assim seriam duas bocas a menos para alimentar.", " perda da ingenuidade", "Sua mãe havia morrido ", "Furiosa, a madrasta mandou um de seus servos sequestrar Branca de Neve e matá-la na floresta, trazendo o seu coração como prova de sua morte.", "A madrasta e as novas “irmãs” tratavam Cinderela muito mal e, depois da morte do patriarca, as três passaram a obrigá-la a dormir no sótão, fazer todo o serviço da casa e vestir-se de farrapos." devem ser suprimidos.

level_sentence_pt:
2;"A crise é consequência de uma demanda bilionária da China que compra as peles destes animais para fazer ejiao, um elixir que promete vitalidade, entre outros benefícios para saúde, segundo os preceitos milenares da Medicina Tradicional Chinesa (MTC)."
3;"A demanda pelo colágeno produzido a partir da pele do jumento já provocou o risco de desaparecimento desses animais na África, continente de onde são originários, e ameaça a espécie no Brasil, onde, segundo cientistas, houve uma redução de 94% da população de jumentos nas últimas décadas."
3;"Segundo a ONG internacional The Donkey Sanctuary, de 2018 a 2024, pelo menos 248 mil jumentos foram abatidos apenas na Bahia, único Estado com três frigoríficos autorizados pelo Serviço de Inspeção Federal (SIF) para realizar esse tipo de atividade."
3;"Ameaça de extinção Celebrado na China como um dos tesouros da sua cultura, o aumento da demanda de ejiao tem provocado a diminuição do rebanho não só no Brasil e na Ásia, mas na África, onde o comércio de pele de jumento foi proibido em 2024 com uma moratória de 15 anos para o abate, em uma decisão unânime de 55 chefes de Estado reunidos na Cúpula da União Africana."
3;"O objetivo das pesquisas é oferecer uma alternativa rastreável, segura e sustentável ao mercado chinês, mas principalmente tornar desnecessário o abate, a extinção e o sofrimento de jumentos, vítimas de uma alta demanda e de uma prática extrativista."
3;"A grande inovação está na forma de produzirmos o colágeno idêntico ao do jumento, que é codificado pelo DNA do animal e que é então a mesma proteína, mas de uma forma desacoplada da matança do animal que é hoje a única forma de obtenção desse colágeno", explica a cientista que tem pós-doutorado em Bem-estar Animal, pelo Instituto ILVO (Bélgica)."
3;"Continuaremos tendo um produto para exportação, mas sem dizimar nossos animais, sem causar crueldade contra eles e também com uma pegada ambiental muito menor", diz a pesquisadora."
3;"No Congresso, há alguns projetos de lei tramitando para proibir o abate de cavalos, equídeos, mulas e jumentos no Brasil, uma prática que hoje é permitida por uma lei de 1984."
3;"Outras frentes de pesquisa A união dos cientistas começou a partir de uma tragédia em fevereiro de 2019, quando uma denúncia anônima levou à descoberta que 200 jumentos tinham morrido de fome em uma fazenda de Canudos, a 372 km de Salvador."
3;"Além deles, outros 800 caminhavam para o mesmo fim enquanto esperavam o abate para serem exportados para a China."
3;"O caso levou a outros similares, como em Itapetininga, também na Bahia, onde se soube que meses antes outras centenas de jumentos tinham morrido de fome e sede."
3;"Nosso trabalho é também estudar este ecótipo, fazer um melhoramento genético associado à raça nordestina, para ter um controle populacional que não traga de volta a justificativa de capturá-los para o abate por demandas como a da China", diz Escodro."
3;"O abandono destes animais e a sua perseguição, explica Barnabé, tem criado outros problemas graves com riscos sanitários identificados no abate, como casos de mormo em humanos, uma doença infecciosa que causa pneumonia, dores musculares e pode levar à morte."
sentence_en:
"The crisis is a consequence of a billion-dollar demand from China, which buys the skins of these animals to make ejiao, an elixir that promises vitality, among other health benefits, according to the ancient precepts of Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM)."
"The demand for collagen produced from donkey skin has already caused the risk of these animals disappearing in Africa, the continent where they originate, and threatens the species in Brazil, where, according to scientists, there has been a 94% reduction in the donkey population in recent decades."
"According to the international NGO The Donkey Sanctuary, from 2018 to 2024, at least 248,000 donkeys were slaughtered in Bahia alone, the only state with three slaughterhouses authorized by the Federal Inspection Service (SIF) to carry out this type of activity."
"Threat of extinction Celebrated in China as one of the treasures of its culture, the increase in demand for ejiao has caused the herd to decline not only in Brazil and Asia, but in Africa, where the donkey skin trade was banned in 2024 with a 15-year moratorium on slaughter, in a unanimous decision by 55 heads of state gathered at the African Union Summit."
"The objective of the research is to offer a traceable, safe and sustainable alternative to the Chinese market, but mainly to make the slaughter, extinction and suffering of donkeys, victims of high demand and extractive practices, unnecessary."
"The great innovation is in the way we produce collagen identical to that of donkeys, which is encoded by the animal's DNA and which is then the same protein, but in a way that is uncoupled from the killing of the animal, which is currently the only way to obtain this collagen", explains the scientist who has a post-doctorate in Animal Welfare, from the ILVO Institute (Belgium)."
"We will continue to have a product for export, but without decimating our animals, without causing cruelty to them and also with a much smaller environmental footprint", says the researcher."
"In Congress, there are some bills being processed to prohibit the slaughter of horses, equids, mules and donkeys in Brazil, a practice that is now permitted by a 1984 law."
"Other research fronts The union of scientists began after a tragedy in February 2019, when an anonymous complaint led to the discovery that 200 donkeys had died of hunger on a farm in Canudos, 372 km from Salvador."
"In addition to them, another 800 were heading towards the same end while awaiting slaughter to be exported to China."
"The case led to other similar cases, such as in Itapetininga, also in Bahia, where it was discovered that months earlier hundreds of donkeys had died of hunger and thirst."
"Our work is also to study this ecotype, to carry out genetic improvement associated with the northeastern breed, to have population control that does not bring back the justification of capturing them for slaughter due to demands like that of China", says Escodro."
"The abandonment of these animals and their persecution, explains Barnabé, has created other serious problems with health risks identified during slaughter, such as cases of glanders in humans, an infectious disease that causes pneumonia, muscle pain and can lead to death."
comment: O texto não é adequado para uma avaliação em larga escala, pois trata de uma invenção brasileira que promete salvar os jumentos da extinção no Nordeste. Pela leitura, é possível entender que os jumentos estão quase extintos devido à exploração do animal, que é morto e tem sua pele exportada para a China.
O texto não é adequado para uma avaliação em larga escala, pois trata de um menino que tem autismo, tema que não é abordado nas avaliações.

sentence_en:
"What is parental alienation: understand the impacts and ways to deal with the problem A situation can occur when the father, mother, grandparents or new partners of the person responsible for the child speak badly about the other part of the family, prevent contact or manipulate the child."
"When a relationship comes to an end or someone responsible for a child dies, disagreements do not always end together."

"Parental alienation is traumatic for young children Among the behaviors that constitute parental alienation are: Constantly speaking badly about the other person responsible; Preventing or hindering the child's contact with their father or mother; Omitting important information, such as school report cards or medical exams; Making false reports to keep the other person away from living with the child; Changing cities without justification to make contact with the other side of the family difficult."
